mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending capacity over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:November 2016 payroll level at 131,028 thousand persons
regime_hint: post-crisis labor recovery, any regime with positive employment trend
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following months with PAYEMS growth > 150k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at or near the zero lower bound (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) constrains monetary policy accommodation capacity, signaling that future rate increases are likely imminent. This expectation of tightening produces negative equity returns as discount rates rise and growth expectations moderate over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 2+ consecutive months
regime_hint: zero lower bound regime with anticipated policy normalization
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based consumer demand strength and construction activity, which translates into positive corporate earnings revisions and equity market gains within 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to move in tandem; isolated strength in one series is insufficient.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 2% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 389,355 million dollars and HOUST at 549 thousand units as of September 2016
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized consumer and housing demand
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following joint expansions are not significantly higher than returns following isolated single-series expansions (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month signal deteriorating manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side weakness, which constrains corporate profit margins and produces negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, which depresses equity valuations through lower earnings expectations and reduced discretionary spending over the subsequent 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.3pp over any 3-month rolling window
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following UNRATE spikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1%, creating a negative real rate environment that erodes bond yields and equity risk premiums. This regime produces positive equity returns as investors seek real asset exposure over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 224.433 and FEDFUNDS at 0.1% as of October 2016
regime_hint: negative real rates with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in negative real rate regimes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter at an annualized rate below 1.5% signals economic stagnation and weak aggregate demand, which compresses equity valuations through lower earnings growth expectations over the subsequent 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,438.8 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q2 2016
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following low-growth quarters are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate month-over-month by more than 3% while retail sales (RSAFS) remain flat or decline, signaling a divergence where construction activity outpaces consumer demand. This imbalance produces negative equity returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate inventory buildup and margin compression in cyclical sectors.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 3% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 549 thousand units and RSAFS at 389,355 million dollars as of September 2016
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following divergent expansions are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).
